enonce.texte:
Exercise 1: Match the school subjects with their names.

Relie chaque matière scolaire à son nom en anglais.

Bloom's level: Remember (Se souvenir)
images: A classroom scene showing: a student writing on a chalkboard with numbers (Maths), a student reading a book with a globe (Geography), a student doing a science experiment with beakers (Science), a student painting on an easel (Art), a student playing football (PE), a student reading a novel (English Literature)

questions.texte: Match the subjects: Maths, English, Science, Art, PE, History
questions.reponse: Maths = Mathematics, English = English Language, Science = Sciences, Art = Arts, PE = Physical Education, History = History

sousQuestions.texte: What subject is about numbers and calculations?
What subject is about painting and drawing?
What subject is about sports and physical activities?

intitule: Days of the Week and Ordinal Numbers

enonce.texte:
Exercise 2: Days of the week and ordinal numbers.

Complète les phrases avec le jour de la semaine ou le nombre ordinal correct.

Bloom's level: Understand (Comprendre)

questions.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct day or ordinal number.

sousQuestions.texte: Monday is the ______ day of the week. (first / second / third)
We have English on ______ and Friday.
Saturday is the ______ day of the week. (sixth / seventh / eighth)
We don't have school on ______ and Sunday.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 3: Read the timetable and answer the questions.

Voici l'emploi du temps de Marie pour la semaine. Lis-le attentivement et réponds aux questions.

TimeMondayTuesdayWednesdayThursdayFriday
8:00 - 9:00MathsEnglishScienceMathsEnglish
9:00 - 10:00EnglishScienceMathsArtScience
10:00 - 11:00HistoryMathsPEScienceHistory

Bloom's level: Analyze (Analyser)

questions.texte: Answer the questions based on the timetable.

sousQuestions.texte: What subject does Marie have on Monday at 8:00?
When does Marie have PE? (Day and time)
How many English lessons does Marie have in a week?
sousQuestions.reponse: She has three English lessons: Monday at 9:00, Tuesday at 8:00, and Friday at 8:00.

sousQuestions.texte: What subject does Marie have on Thursday at 9:00?

intitule: Asking and Answering About School Subjects

enonce.texte:
Exercise 4: Ask and answer questions about school subjects.

Complete the dialogues between two students, Paul and Amina.

Bloom's level: Apply (Appliquer)

questions.texte: Complete the dialogue with the correct words.

sousQuestions.texte: Paul: '______ is your favourite subject, Amina?'
Amina: 'My favourite subject is ______. I love painting.'
Paul: '______ do you have Art?'
Amina: 'I have Art on ______ and Friday.'
Paul: '______ is your favourite subject?'

enonce.texte:
Exercise 5: Write about your school subjects.

Rédige un court paragraphe (4-5 phrases) sur tes matières préférées et ton emploi du temps.

Utilise les expressions suivantes :

  • My favourite subject is ...
  • I have ... on ...
  • I like ... because ...
  • I don't like ... because ...

Bloom's level: Create (Créer)

questions.texte: Write 4-5 sentences about your school subjects.
questions.reponse: Example answer: My favourite subject is English. I have English on Monday, Tuesday and Friday. I like English because it is interesting. I have Maths on Monday and Wednesday. I don't like Maths because it is difficult.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 6: Listen to the dialogue and answer the questions.

Écoute le dialogue entre deux élèves et réponds aux questions.

Dialogue (Teacher reads aloud):

"Sarah: Hi John! What's your favourite subject?
John: Hi Sarah! My favourite subject is Science. I love doing experiments!
Sarah: When do you have Science?
John: I have Science on Tuesday and Thursday at 9:00. What about you?
Sarah: I prefer History and English. I have History on Monday and Friday. English is my favourite!
John: When do you have English?
Sarah: I have English on Monday, Wednesday and Friday!"

Bloom's level: Understand (Comprendre) and Analyze (Analyser)
images: Two students (one boy, one girl) sitting at a desk in a classroom talking, with books and notebooks on the table, and a chalkboard with school subjects in the background

questions.texte: Answer the questions based on the dialogue.

sousQuestions.texte: What is John's favourite subject?
When does John have Science?
sousQuestions.reponse: He has Science on Tuesday and Thursday at 9:00.

sousQuestions.texte: What is Sarah's favourite subject?
When does Sarah have English?
sousQuestions.reponse: She has English on Monday, Wednesday and Friday.

sousQuestions.texte: Which subject does Sarah have on Monday?
sousQuestions.reponse: Sarah has History and English on Monday.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 7: Create and present your ideal timetable.

Crée un emploi du temps idéal pour la semaine. Choisis 6 matières scolaires et place-les dans un tableau.

Ensuite, réponds aux questions sur ton emploi du temps.

Bloom's level: Create (Créer) and Evaluate (Évaluer)

questions.texte: Create a timetable for the week with your favourite subjects.
questions.reponse: Example answer: Monday: Maths, English, History; Tuesday: Science, PE, Art; Wednesday: Maths, English, Science; Thursday: History, Art, PE; Friday: English, Maths, Science.

sousQuestions.texte: Write your timetable in a table.
sousQuestions.reponse: Students should create a table with days of the week and subjects.

sousQuestions.texte: What is your favourite subject and why?
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: My favourite subject is English because it is interesting and I like learning new words.

sousQuestions.texte: What subject do you have on Wednesday?
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: I have Maths, English and Science on Wednesday.

sousQuestions.texte: Present your timetable to your classmates.
sousQuestions.reponse: Oral presentation: Students present their timetable in 3-4 sentences.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 8: Form questions about school subjects.

Transforme les phrases affirmatives en questions en utilisant What ou When.

Bloom's level: Analyze (Analyser)

sousQuestions.texte: Statement: My favourite subject is Science. → Question: ______?
Statement: I have Maths on Tuesday and Thursday. → Question: ______?
Statement: We have PE on Wednesday at 10:00. → Question: ______?
Statement: I like English because it's interesting. → Question: ______?

enonce.texte:
Exercise 9: Interview your classmate about school subjects.

Pose 5 questions à ton camarade sur ses matières préférées et son emploi du temps. Note ses réponses et prépare une courte présentation.

Questions à poser :

  1. What's your favourite subject?
  2. When do you have it?
  3. Why do you like it?
  4. What subject do you not like?
  5. When do you have English class?

Bloom's level: Apply (Appliquer) and Evaluate (Évaluer)
images: Two students sitting at a desk in a classroom, one holding a notebook and a pen, interviewing the other about school subjects, with a timetable on the wall

questions.texte: Interview your classmate and write down their answers.
questions.reponse: Example answers: 1. My favourite subject is English. 2. I have English on Monday, Wednesday and Friday. 3. I like it because it's interesting and fun. 4. I don't like Maths because it's difficult. 5. I have English at 8:00.

sousQuestions.texte: Write your classmate's answers in complete sentences.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: My classmate's favourite subject is English. He has English on Monday, Wednesday and Friday. He likes it because it's interesting and fun. He doesn't like Maths because it's difficult. He has English at 8:00.

sousQuestions.texte: Present your findings to the class.
sousQuestions.reponse: Oral presentation: Students present their classmate's answers in 5-6 sentences.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 10: School subjects quiz.

Réponds aux questions suivantes sur les matières scolaires et l'emploi du temps.

Bloom's level: Remember (Se souvenir), Understand (Comprendre) and Analyze (Analyser)

questions.texte: Answer the following questions about school subjects.

sousQuestions.texte: What subject teaches you about the past?
What day comes after Tuesday?
What is the 5th day of the week?
If you have PE on Thursday and Saturday, how many PE lessons do you have in a week?
Why do you like your favourite subject? Give two reasons.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: I like English because it is interesting and I like speaking English.
